title: SyncState
description:
`skills/<id>/sync.json` — the durable client sync state (the four-state currency machine's memory).
**Fully pinned.** The four states (CURRENT / BEHIND / DRAFT / DIVERGED) are *derived* from these
fields, never stored.

applied.description: Highest generation actually MATERIALIZED — advances only after a successful swap (`≤ observed`).

base_commit.description: The commit the working tree derives from (= the applied commit when clean).

held.description: A transient local pin (a `pull <skill>@<hash>` go-back) suppressing one auto fast-forward.

observed.description:
Highest AUTHENTICATED (signature-verified) generation ever seen — the anti-rollback floor and
the retry target. Only a signed record raises it.

recorded.description:
Per observed generation, the commit it carried — a reused tuple with a *different* commit is a
loud ALARM; same tuple + same commit is a no-op.

work_hash.description: sha256 (lowercase hex) of the current harness-dir bytes (recomputed; cheap).

Generation.description:
The internal anti-replay counter `(epoch, seq)`. NEVER rendered to a user; the
agent consumes `expected`/`current` on a CONFLICT.

RecordedTuple.description:
One `(generation → commit)` record in [`SyncState::recorded`]. (A list, not a JSON-object map,
because the key is the `(epoch,seq)` pair.)

commit_id.description: The commit (`version_id`) seen at this generation.
